seoTitle: Cement Mills vs Centenary Heights — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cement Mills and Centenary Heights. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cement Mills vs Centenary Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cement Mills and Centenary Heights.
heading: Cement Mills vs Centenary Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cement Mills, Queensland and Centenary Heights, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Centenary Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Cement Mills (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cement Mills or Centenary Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cement Mills has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Centenary Heights scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cement Mills scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Centenary Heights scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cement Mills or Centenary Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Centenary Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
